title: Angry Dad The Movie

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that defines the character. High character integration and economy but not original anymore.

jokes.text: Gah!
jokes.punchline: Another Homer exclamation of annoyance
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer vocalization. Less iconic than 'D'oh!' but still character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Let's get this party started.
jokes.setup: Family is gone and Bart is alone
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual attitude about being unsupervised
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's mischievous nature when left alone, but the line itself is fairly generic.

jokes.text: Bart's been alone for 23 minutes. Let's get this yo on the road, people. Wait, I haven't chosen my mix-in. Okay, I'll take some of that. Well, that's my tip jar. Mix it in!
jokes.setup: Bart ordering at an ice cream shop
jokes.punchline: Demanding money from the tip jar be mixed into his ice cream
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's bratty entitlement. The tip jar mix-in is an absurd but believable Bart demand.

jokes.text: Whoa, mama!
jokes.setup: Bart reacting to something (likely the ice cream)
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated appreciation
jokes.explanation: Standard excited exclamation, not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: I love Saturday!
jokes.punchline: Simple expression of weekend joy
jokes.explanation: Basic character moment showing Homer's simple pleasures, but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I'm bored. That's it, boy! I'm eating your yogurt! You ate his yogurt in the car. He didn't know that. Stupid kid. All you do is cost me money. Money I could be wasting.
jokes.setup: Family arguing, Homer threatening to eat Bart's yogurt as punishment
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission that he wastes money anyway
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - his accidental honesty about wasting money undermines his own argument about Bart costing money.

jokes.text: Hey, remember the guy who drove you to school that one time.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing about making the family wealthy
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic attempt to insert himself as important to Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - desperately trying to claim credit for minimal parental effort. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: I legally forced him to say semi.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining Angry Dad is semi-autobiographical
jokes.punchline: Homer's admission he threatened legal action over accuracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke with perfect economy. Shows Homer's vanity and litigiousness while being meta about cartoon accuracy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Thank you! Oh, don't thank me. Thank Hollywood for being completely out of good ideas.
jokes.setup: Herman offering to make Angry Dad into a movie
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate commentary on Hollywood adaptations
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Hollywood's desperation for content. Self-aware and accurate.

jokes.text: My little Roman Polanski. Homer! What? What's wrong with being Roman Polanski? He what? You monster!
jokes.setup: Herman comparing Bart to famous director Roman Polanski
jokes.punchline: Homer's horror when he learns what Polanski did
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Polanski's crimes. Homer's innocent enthusiasm turning to horror works well.

jokes.text: I've always loved cartoons, going way back to the real early SpongeBobs.
jokes.setup: Herman trying to establish his cartoon credentials
jokes.punchline: Treating SpongeBob as if it's ancient animation history
jokes.explanation: Good joke about how relatively recent things get treated as ancient. Shows character's shallow knowledge.

jokes.text: What the hell are you making chairs for? In this family, we sit on the ground!
jokes.setup: Herman explaining everyone has an angry dad
jokes.punchline: His father's irrational anger about chair-making
jokes.explanation: Good parallel to Angry Dad concept. Absurd family tradition of sitting on ground is funny and fits the theme.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, I'm in charge of the team that animates your ass.
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'animates your ass' - both the character and Homer himself
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay about animating Homer's posterior vs. the character. Economy is good but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Wow. Whoa! Uh! Soda? No, thanks. Eh. More for me.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrating anger for the animators
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual transition from rage to politely declining soda to drinking it anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's instant mood changes and selfishness with the soda.

jokes.text: He blew out his voice screaming... about how he never got paid. The first time around.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the original voice actor can't return
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on voice actors not being paid fairly
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about animation industry practices. Good structure with the pause before 'The first time around.'

jokes.text: No, Dad, don't say 'pained noises.' You make pained noises. Oh, okay. Lenny, hit me with that chair.
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is to have Lenny actually hit him with a chair
jokes.explanation: Great character moment. Homer's literal interpretation and willingness to get hurt for authenticity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: To make this film, I had to miss countless hours of school. And there was also some hardship.
jokes.setup: Bart talking about sacrifices for the film
jokes.punchline: Treating missing school as a hardship rather than a benefit
jokes.explanation: Good irony since Bart would love missing school. Shows him trying to sound professional and suffering for his art.

jokes.text: For God's sake, turn it off! Turn it off!
jokes.punchline: Immediate negative reaction to the film
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to bad movie, but works to show how terrible Bart's film is.

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, boy. Everyone makes mistakes. Yours is just public and expensive.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort disappointed Bart
jokes.punchline: Backhanded comfort emphasizing how visible and costly Bart's failure is
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer line. Perfect structure with comforting setup undermined by harsh reality. Very quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Name one more. Taylor Hackford. Well, I don't know who that is, but I'm convinced.
jokes.setup: Lisa naming famous directors who started with shorts
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting he doesn't know the name but being convinced anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart being easily swayed by authority even when he doesn't understand it.

jokes.text: Your animated short, Angry Dad, or as we call it in the pizza English-- Papa Apoplectica
jokes.setup: Luigi announcing the Golden Globe nomination
jokes.punchline: Italian translation that maintains the angry father concept
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits Luigi's character. 'Papa Apoplectica' is a clever Italian version that sounds authentic.

jokes.text: Sir Anthony, your pizza-- She's a-gonna be late. Damn you. Damn you to hell!
jokes.setup: Luigi delivering bad news about pizza timing
jokes.punchline: Anthony Hopkins' dramatic overreaction to late pizza
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. Hopkins' theatrical response to mundane pizza delay fits his dramatic reputation.

jokes.text: I have seen all of your movies-- except Cars-- and I can't believe my brother is in the same category as the digital Rembrandts of my generation.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment excluding Cars from worthy Pixar films
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture dig at Cars being considered Pixar's weakest film. Fits Lisa's intellectual snobbishness.

jokes.text: ♪ You've got an enemy ♪ ♪ You better have nine lives because we have eight knives ♪ ♪ Yes, you've got an enemy ♪
jokes.punchline: Threatening song about having eight knives for nine lives
jokes.explanation: Absurd threatening song that contrasts with Pixar's family-friendly image. The knife/lives math adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Hi. Yeah, it's me, Ricky Gervais.
jokes.punchline: His assumption that everyone knows who he is
jokes.explanation: Good character work capturing Gervais's self-importance and assumption of recognition.

jokes.text: Tanqueray gin and tonic, please. I'm paid to drink that in my movies. The amount isn't important. A lot to you-- let's be honest. Not so much to me.
jokes.punchline: Condescending explanation about product placement and wealth disparity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gervais characterization - arrogant, condescending, and self-aggrandizing while pretending to be humble.

jokes.text: The ten? That was there before. It was not! Who else in here is going to tip ten dollars?! Keith Urban? Helen Mirren? I don't think so.
jokes.punchline: Gervais getting increasingly agitated about tip credit and naming other celebrities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gervais's desperation for recognition even for basic human decency like tipping.

jokes.text: Helen. Helen. She can't hear me. Helen! Mirren! Who does she think she is? Fur coat, no knickers.
jokes.setup: Gervais trying to get Helen Mirren's attention
jokes.punchline: His frustration leading to crude British expression about her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Gervais's neediness. The British expression adds authenticity to his character.

jokes.text: It's great to be here with all the old friends I haven't seen since rehab. No, no, I'm just kidding. None of you are my friends.
jokes.punchline: Dark joke about rehab followed by admission he doesn't like anyone
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Brand - self-deprecating about addiction but also misanthropic. Fits his public persona.

jokes.text: I have many people to thank. Angry Dad's voice is part Walter Matthau-- Hey, Felix-- part Little Richard-- Whoo! and a little bit of Snoopy when he cries:
jokes.punchline: His detailed breakdown of voice influences including Snoopy
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Homer's thought process. The mix of highbrow (Matthau), lowbrow (Little Richard), and cartoon (Snoopy) is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: This is insane! He didn't show up for work, he badmouthed the movie on Jimmy Fallon, and now he's hogging all the credit!
jokes.punchline: Bart's frustrated list of Homer's terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing typical Homer behavior - lazy, disloyal, and then claiming credit anyway.

jokes.text: I'd like to thank Mr. Speedy Pants Repair for sewing up my pants just before the ceremony and the woman who has inspired me every day for the past 25 years, the cute one from The Bangles!
jokes.punchline: Thanking random pants repair service and vaguely referencing a Bangles member
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer speech - random mundane thanks mixed with inappropriate personal revelation about celebrity crush.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm wearing my retainer.
jokes.punchline: Awkward TMI about dental appliances
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Lisa's social awkwardness. The retainer detail is random and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: ♪ I'm going to the Oscars not as a seat-filler ♪ ♪ I'll get a gift basket, but I won't declare it. ♪
jokes.punchline: Song about not being a seat-filler and tax evasion
jokes.explanation: Good insider knowledge about Oscar gift basket tax issues and seat-fillers. Shows Bart's excitement and mild criminality.

jokes.text: My lame-o sister Lisa. And Lisa, who are you wearing? I believe it's from Lamps Plus.
jokes.setup: Red carpet interview asking about Lisa's outfit
jokes.punchline: Lisa wearing something from a lighting store instead of designer fashion
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of red carpet fashion questions. Lamps Plus as couture is absurd and fits the family's working-class reality.

jokes.text: Homer? I don't know. L.A.'s a world-class city with a lot to offer. He's probably enjoying the sights.
jokes.punchline: Bart's optimistic assumption immediately followed by Homer in trouble
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual joke. Bart's faith in Homer being immediately undercut by reality.

jokes.text: Yo-yo-yo! Why you messing with Simon Rodeo's folk-art masterpiece?! Check it out! That's Angry Dad! Hey, why ain't you at the Oscars, A.D.?
jokes.setup: Gang finding Homer at graffiti wall
jokes.punchline: Gang member recognizing Angry Dad character and calling Homer 'A.D.'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with gang members being fans of the cartoon and giving Homer a street nickname.

jokes.text: My son gave me this list of awesome things to do in L.A. 'Chatsworth'? That's where the 118 meets Topanga Canyon, fool! Someone must not want you at that kudo-cast, yo.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's not at Oscars
jokes.punchline: Gang member realizing Bart deliberately sent Homer to wrong locations
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that Bart orchestrated Homer's absence. Gang member being more perceptive than Homer adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I home free as long as this Oscar show is tight and fast-paced. Sound mixers may not be glamorous, but they make us sound so good. Without them, we'd be up a creek without a decibel.
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by long-winded presenter with terrible pun
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing with Bart's wish immediately undercut. The 'decibel' pun is groan-worthy which is the point.

jokes.text: Turn it off, turn it off! For God's sake, turn it off!
jokes.punchline: Same reaction as to Bart's original movie
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier audience reaction to Bart's movie. Shows even professional entertainment can be unwatchable.

jokes.text: When most people think of animated movies, they think of 40 minutes or more, but there is another type of animated film: 40 minutes or less.
jokes.punchline: Treating the difference between features and shorts as profound discovery
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Oscar presenter pompousness, treating obvious distinctions as deep insights.

jokes.text: The Tabasco brothers got Frenchy! We're gonna have to leave the kitchen. I don't want to go! I don't want to go!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic condiment warfare and emotional attachment to kitchen
jokes.explanation: Good Pixar parody applying their emotional formula to condiments. Absurd but recognizably in their style.

jokes.text: My God, I'm empty! Don't let 'em put pencils in me!
jokes.punchline: Fear of being used as pencil holder when empty
jokes.explanation: Clever existential fear for a condiment container. Dark but funny take on object mortality.

jokes.text: I was born a Rollin' 80 and I'll die a Rollin' 80!
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic gang loyalty declaration
jokes.explanation: Good parody of gang loyalty tropes

jokes.text: Hey, you two are honorary members for life. That means any other gang sees you, they'll kill you. I was born a Rollin' 80 and I'll die a Rollin' 80!
jokes.setup: Gang making Homer and companion honorary members
jokes.punchline: The honor comes with death threats from rival gangs
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony about gang 'honors' being dangerous. Homer's earnest acceptance adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Homemade Marmite?! And made by a woman with such gentle hands.
jokes.setup: British characters discussing tea and sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Excessive excitement about homemade Marmite and creepy focus on hands
jokes.explanation: Good British stereotype humor. The hand fixation adds creepy undertone to the politeness.

jokes.text: Good luck, Mr. Park. There will be no shame in losing to you. Oh, that's very sweet of you. Thank you for saying so. No worries. I'll just stick 'em back on. I'm more clay than man now.
jokes.punchline: Claymation character's existential crisis about being more clay than human
jokes.explanation: Excellent existential humor about claymation. 'More clay than man now' is a perfect line about artistic sacrifice.

jokes.text: Ah! Damn, that's good!
jokes.setup: Angry Dad cartoon clip during Oscar presentation
jokes.punchline: Angry Dad's satisfied reaction to something
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angry Dad's occasional satisfaction. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Wait, one last moment about me. Every night, I dream that my teeth fly out of my mouth.
jokes.punchline: Random TMI about anxiety dreams
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hollywood narcissism and inappropriate oversharing at awards shows.

jokes.text: I only wish he were here, and not at Cerritos Auto Square.
jokes.punchline: Specific, mundane location reference
jokes.explanation: Good specificity adding humor to emotional moment

jokes.text: I mean, isn't the idea of one person taking credit for an entire movie the stupidest thing you ever heard? You make a lot of good points, Bart, a lot of good points. You're a very thoughtful kid. You remind me of Deborah Kerr in Black Narcissus.
jokes.punchline: Spielberg's condescending response with random obscure film reference
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on film industry credit-taking. Spielberg's patronizing response and obscure reference fits his character.

jokes.text: Hey, they're, like, five bucks on eBay.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Oscars are worthless on resale market
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and deflation of Oscar prestige. Bart's casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're thinking, 'Oh, my word! He's been making me laugh all these years, but now he's moving me as well! I'm gonna have to put him in my new film!'
jokes.setup: Gervais continuing his pitch to Ridley Scott
jokes.punchline: Imagining Scott's thoughts in exaggerated terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy showing Gervais's complete lack of self-awareness and desperate need for validation.

jokes.text: What do you think? I'm a seat-filler. Well, why couldn't you have said that a minute ago? Don't take any of my ideas.
jokes.setup: Gervais asking for opinion after long pitch
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was talking to a seat-filler, then paranoia about idea theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the Gervais sequence. Perfect misdirection and his paranoia about idea theft is the perfect ending beat.

tmdb_overview: Bart's film about an angry father quickly becomes a critical favorite, winning him several awards.
